questions.q: Paragraph:A voltage source $\mathrm{V}=\mathrm{V}_0 \sin (100 \mathrm{t})$ is connected to a black box in which there can be either one element out of L,C,R or any two of them connected in series. At steady state the variation of current in the circuit and the source voltage are plotted together with time, using an oscilloscope, as shown.Question:Values of the parameters of the elements, present in the black box are :
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A circuit shown in the figure in which $\mathrm{k}_1$ is closed and $\mathrm{k}_2$ is open. Inductor L can be connected to capacitor $\mathrm{C}_1$ by closing switch $\mathrm{k}_2$ and opening $\mathrm{k}_1$.
Question: At $t=0$, when capacitors are fully charged, switch $k_1$ is opened and switch $k_2$ is closed, so that inductor is connected in series with capacitor $\mathrm{C}_1$. The maximum charge will appear on capacitor $\mathrm{C}_1$ at time $t$ is
questions.solution:
For long time capacitor gets full charged and charge on each capacitor must be same.
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{\mathrm{Q}}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{Q}}{3}=20 \\ & \mathrm{Q}=24 \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & {\left[\omega=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{LC}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-6}}}=\frac{10}{2}=50 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\right]} \\ & \mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{t})=24 \sin \omega \mathrm{t} \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & \mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{t})=(24 \sin \omega \mathrm{t}) \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & 500 \mathrm{t}=\frac{\pi}{2}(-1)^{\mathrm{n}}+\pi \mathrm{n} \\ & \mathrm{t}=\frac{\mathrm{n} \pi}{500}+(-1)^{\mathrm{n}} \frac{\pi}{1000} \\ & \mathrm{E}_{\max }=\frac{\mathrm{Q}_0^2}{2 C}=\frac{24 \times 10^{-3} \times 24 \times 10^{-6}}{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-6}}=144 \times 10^{-6} \text { Joule }=0.144 \mathrm{~mJ}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q:
Paragraph: A circuit shown in the figure in which $\mathrm{k}_1$ is closed and $\mathrm{k}_2$ is open. Inductor L can be connected to capacitor $\mathrm{C}_1$ by closing switch $\mathrm{k}_2$ and opening $\mathrm{k}_1$.
Question: The maximum energy across inductor will be
questions.solution:
For long time capacitor gets full charged and charge on each capacitor must be same.
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{\mathrm{Q}}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{Q}}{3}=20 \\ & \mathrm{Q}=24 \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & {\left[\omega=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{LC}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-6}}}=\frac{10}{2}=50 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\right]} \\ & \mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{t})=24 \sin \omega \mathrm{t} \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & \mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{t})=(24 \sin \omega \mathrm{t}) \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & 500 \mathrm{t}=\frac{\pi}{2}(-1)^{\mathrm{n}}+\pi \mathrm{n} \\ & \mathrm{t}=\frac{\mathrm{n} \pi}{500}+(-1)^{\mathrm{n}} \frac{\pi}{1000} \\ & \mathrm{E}_{\max }=\frac{\mathrm{Q}_0^2}{2 C}=\frac{24 \times 10^{-3} \times 24 \times 10^{-6}}{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-6}}=144 \times 10^{-6} \text { Joule }=0.144 \mathrm{~mJ}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph:A voltage source $\mathrm{V}=\mathrm{V}_0 \sin (100 \mathrm{t})$ is connected to a black box in which there can be either one element out of L,C,R or any two of them connected in series. At steady state the variation of current in the circuit and the source voltage are plotted together with time, using an oscilloscope, as shown.Question:If AC source is removed, the circuit is shorted and then at $t=0$, a battery of constant EMF is connected across the black box. The current in the circuit will-
questions.solution: For DC circuit $\mathrm{i}=\mathrm{i}_0 e^{-\frac{t}{R C}}$ and $\mathrm{RC}=0.01 \mathrm{sec}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: A circuit shown in the figure in which $k_1$ is closed and $k_2$ is open. Inductor L can be connected to capacitor $\mathrm{C}_1$ by closing switch $\mathrm{k}_2$ and opening $\mathrm{k}_1$.
Question: Let switch $k_1$ is closed and $k_2$ is opened for long time. The charge on capacitor $\mathrm{C}_2$ will be
questions.solution:
For long time capacitor gets full charged and charge on each capacitor must be same.
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{\mathrm{Q}}{2}+\frac{\mathrm{Q}}{3}=20 \\ & \mathrm{Q}=24 \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & {\left[\omega=\frac{1}{\sqrt{\mathrm{LC}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-6}}}=\frac{10}{2}=50 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\right]} \\ & \mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{t})=24 \sin \omega \mathrm{t} \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & \mathrm{Q}(\mathrm{t})=(24 \sin \omega \mathrm{t}) \mu \mathrm{C} \\ & 500 \mathrm{t}=\frac{\pi}{2}(-1)^{\mathrm{n}}+\pi \mathrm{n} \\ & \mathrm{t}=\frac{\mathrm{n} \pi}{500}+(-1)^{\mathrm{n}} \frac{\pi}{1000} \\ & \mathrm{E}_{\max }=\frac{\mathrm{Q}_0^2}{2 C}=\frac{24 \times 10^{-3} \times 24 \times 10^{-6}}{2 \times 2 \times 10^{-6}}=144 \times 10^{-6} \text { Joule }=0.144 \mathrm{~mJ}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Two capacitors of capacitance $C$ and $3 C$ are charged to potential difference $\mathrm{V}_0$ and $2 \mathrm{~V}_0$ respectively and connected to an inductor of inductance $L$ as shown in the figure. Initially the current in the inductor is zero. Now the switch S is closed.
Question: Potential difference across capacitor of capacitance C when the current in the circuit is maximum
questions.solution: When current is maximum $\frac{\mathrm{di}}{\mathrm{dt}}=0$ $\therefore$ emf across $\mathrm{L}=0$ and potential difference across the capacitor will be same. From conservation of charge $$ \begin{aligned} & 3 C V+C V=6 \mathrm{CV}_0-\mathrm{CV}_0 \\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{V}=\frac{5 \mathrm{CV}}{4} \end{aligned} $$ Loss in energy of capacitor $=$ energy stored in inductor $$ \Rightarrow I_{\max }=\frac{3 V_0}{2} \sqrt{\frac{3 C}{L}} $$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Paragraph: Two capacitors of capacitance $C$ and $3 C$ are charged to potential difference $\mathrm{V}_0$ and $2 \mathrm{~V}_0$ respectively and connected to an inductor of inductance $L$ as shown in the figure. Initially the current in the inductor is zero. Now the switch S is closed.
Question: Potential difference across capacitor of capacitance $3 C$ when the current in the circuit is maximum
questions.solution: When current is maximum $\frac{\mathrm{di}}{\mathrm{dt}}=0$ $\therefore$ emf across $\mathrm{L}=0$ and potential difference across the capacitor will be same. From conservation of charge $$ \begin{aligned} & 3 C V+C V=6 \mathrm{CV}_0-\mathrm{CV}_0 \\ \Rightarrow & \mathrm{V}=\frac{5 \mathrm{CV}}{4} \end{aligned} $$ Loss in energy of capacitor $=$ energy stored in inductor $$ \Rightarrow I_{\max }=\frac{3 V_0}{2} \sqrt{\frac{3 C}{L}} $$
